content.text: **4. Update `slices/active-task-list.md`**

input.content:
# Slice: ActiveTaskList

## Pattern

**View** (State View / Read Model)

## Summary

A read model that projects all events into an ordered list of active
(incomplete, non-deleted) tasks. This powers the main view of the application.

Position data comes from the task list events (TaskAddedToList,
TaskReprioritized, TaskRemovedFromList) rather than from per-task events. When
a TaskAdded event arrives before its corresponding TaskAddedToList event, the
projector assigns a temporary position (max + 1) until the authoritative
position is established.

## Diagram

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    E1["TaskAdded"]:::event
    E2["TaskCompleted"]:::event
    E3["TaskReopened"]:::event
    E4["TaskDescriptionChanged"]:::event
    E5["TaskReprioritized"]:::event
    E6["TaskDeleted"]:::event
    E7["TaskAddedToList"]:::event
    E8["TaskRemovedFromList"]:::event
    VIEW["ActiveTaskList"]:::view

    E1 --> VIEW
    E2 --> VIEW
    E3 --> VIEW
    E4 --> VIEW
    E5 --> VIEW
    E6 --> VIEW
    E7 --> VIEW
    E8 --> VIEW

    classDef event fill:#f59e0b,color:#fff
    classDef view fill:#22c55e,color:#fff
```

## Read Model: ActiveTaskList

### Fields

| Field | Type | Source Event(s) | Description |
|-------|------|----------------|-------------|
| tasks | list | (all events) | Ordered list of active tasks |
| tasks[].task_id | identifier | TaskAdded.task_id | Unique task identifier |
| tasks[].description | text | TaskAdded.description, TaskDescriptionChanged.description | Current description (latest value) |
| tasks[].position | number | TaskAddedToList.position, TaskReprioritized.position | Current priority position (authoritative from task list) |
| completed_count | number | TaskCompleted (+1), TaskReopened (-1), TaskDeleted (if completed, -1) | Count of completed tasks for badge |

### Filters

- Only tasks where **completed = false** AND **deleted = false**.

### Ordering

- By position ascending. Position 1 = highest priority (top of list).

### Event Processing Rules

| Event | Effect on Read Model |
|-------|---------------------|
| TaskAdded | Add new task to the list with a temporary position (max + 1). The task is visible but awaiting authoritative positioning from TaskAddedToList. |
| TaskAddedToList | Set the authoritative position for the task. Shift other tasks as needed to maintain contiguous positions. |
| TaskCompleted | Remove task from active list. Increment completed_count. |
| TaskReopened | Mark task as active again (it will be re-added to the list once the TaskAddedToList automation fires). Decrement completed_count. |
| TaskDescriptionChanged | Update description of matching task. |
| TaskReprioritized | Move task to new position. Shift other tasks to accommodate. |
| TaskRemovedFromList | Remove task from the ordered position list. Shift remaining tasks to close the gap. |
| TaskDeleted | Remove task from active list. If task was completed, decrement completed_count. |

### Information Completeness Check

| Display Element | Source |
|----------------|--------|
| Task description text | TaskAdded.description / TaskDescriptionChanged.description |
| Task order | TaskAddedToList.position / TaskReprioritized.position |
| Completed count badge | TaskCompleted / TaskReopened / TaskDeleted |
| Empty state message | Derived: tasks list is empty |

All fields trace to events. No missing data.

## Wireframe

```
+--------------------------------------------------+
|  My Tasks                        [Completed (3)]  |
+--------------------------------------------------+
|                                                    |
|  [ What needs to be done?            ] [+ Add]    |
|                                                    |
|  ::  Buy groceries                    [v] [ x ]   |
|  ::  Call the dentist                 [v] [ x ]   |
|  ::  Fix the leaky faucet            [v] [ x ]   |
|  ::  Write thank-you notes           [v] [ x ]   |
|                                                    |
+--------------------------------------------------+
```

## GWT Scenarios

### Scenario: TaskAdded adds task with temporary position

**Given** (current projection state):
- ActiveTaskList { tasks: [], completed_count: 0 }

**When** (event to process):
- TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" }

**Then** (resulting projection state):
- ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }], completed_count: 0 }

**Note:** Position 1 is assigned as a temporary position (max + 1, where max is 0 for an empty list). The authoritative position will come from TaskAddedToList.

---

### Scenario: TaskAdded adds task with temporary position to a populated list

**Given** (current projection state):
- ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }, { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 2 }], completed_count: 0 }

**When** (event to process):
- TaskAdded { task_id: "task-003", description: "Fix the leaky faucet" }

**Then** (resulting projection state):
- ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }, { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 2 }, { task_id: "task-003", description: "Fix the leaky faucet", position: 3 }], completed_count: 0 }

**Note:** Temporary position 3 (max + 1). The subsequent TaskAddedToList event will confirm or adjust this position.

---

### Scenario: TaskAddedToList sets authoritative position for a task

**Given** (current projection state):
- ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }, { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 2 }, { task_id: "task-003", description: "Fix the leaky faucet", position: 3 }], completed_count: 0 }

**When** (event to process):
- TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-003", position: 3 }

**Then** (resulting projection state):
- ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }, { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 2 }, { task_id: "task-003", description: "Fix the leaky faucet", position: 3 }], completed_count: 0 }

**Note:** In this case the temporary position matches the authoritative position. The projection is now confirmed.

---

### Scenario: TaskAddedToList places a reopened task at top, shifting others

**Given** (current projection state):
- ActiveTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries", position: 1 }, { task_id: "task-003", description: "Fix the leaky faucet", position: 2 }, { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist", position: 3 }], completed_count: 1 }

**When** (event to process):
- TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-004", position: 1 }

**Then** (resulting projection state):

# Slice: CompleteTask ## Pattern **Command** (State Change) ## Summary The user clicks the checkbox on an active task to mark it as done. The task disappears from the active list and appears in the completed tasks view. An automation then removes the task from the ordered task list (see [RemoveFromTaskList](remove-from-task-list.md)). ## Diagram ```mermaid flowchart LR UI["Active Task List
checkbox on task"] CMD["CompleteTask"]:::command EVT["TaskCompleted"]:::event VIEW1["ActiveTaskList"]:::view VIEW2["CompletedTaskList"]:::view AUTO["Automation:
RemoveFromTaskList"]:::automation UI -->|"task_id"| CMD CMD --> EVT EVT --> VIEW1 EVT --> VIEW2 EVT --> AUTO classDef command fill:#3b82f6,color:#fff classDef event fill:#f59e0b,color:#fff classDef view fill:#22c55e,color:#fff classDef automation fill:#8b5cf6,color:#fff ``` ## Command: CompleteTask | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | task_id | identifier | The task to mark as complete | **Triggered by:** User clicks the [v] checkbox on an active task. **Rejection conditions:** - Task is already completed. ## Event: TaskCompleted | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | task_id | identifier | The task that was completed | **Downstream automation:** When TaskCompleted is recorded, an automation dispatches RemoveFromTaskList to remove the task from the ordered task list. See [RemoveFromTaskList](remove-from-task-list.md). ## Wireframe ``` Before: +--------------------------------------------------+ | My Tasks [Completed (2)] | +--------------------------------------------------+ | | | :: Buy groceries [v] [ x ] | | :: Call the dentist [v] [ x ] | | :: Fix the leaky faucet [v] [ x ] | | | +--------------------------------------------------+ User clicks [v] on "Call the dentist": +--------------------------------------------------+ | My Tasks [Completed (3)] | +--------------------------------------------------+ | | | :: Buy groceries [v] [ x ] | | :: Fix the leaky faucet [v] [ x ] | | | +--------------------------------------------------+ Task disappears from active list, completed count increments. ``` ## Read Model Impact **ActiveTaskList:** Task removed from active list. Completed count incremented. **CompletedTaskList:** Task appears in completed list (most recent first). ## GWT Scenarios ### Scenario: Complete an active task **Given** (prior events): - TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" } - TaskAdded { task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist" } **When** (command): - CompleteTask { task_id: "task-002" } **Then** (events produced): - TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-002" } --- ### Scenario: Reject completing an already-completed task **Given** (prior events): - TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" } - TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-001" } **When** (command): - CompleteTask { task_id: "task-001" } **Then** (error -- no events): - Error: "Task is already completed"

A confirmation dialog appears. If the user confirms, the task is permanently removed from all views. delete button"] UI2["Completed Tasks View
delete button"] DIALOG["Confirmation Dialog"] CMD["DeleteTask"]:::command EVT["TaskDeleted"]:::event VIEW1["ActiveTaskList"]:::view VIEW2["CompletedTaskList"]:::view AUTO["Automation:

The event stream retains the full history, but the UI provides no way to restore a deleted task. **Downstream automation:** When TaskDeleted is recorded for an active task, an automation dispatches RemoveFromTaskList to remove the task from the ordered task list. If user clicks [Cancel], nothing changes. ``` ## Read Model Impact **ActiveTaskList:** Task removed (if it was active). # Slice: AddToTaskList ## Pattern **Automation** ## Summary When a task is created (TaskAdded) or reopened (TaskReopened), it needs to be placed into the ordered task list. This automation listens for those events and dispatches an AddToTaskList command to the task list. - **On TaskAdded:** The task is placed at the bottom of the list (lowest priority). The user can reprioritize it afterward. - **On TaskReopened:** The task is placed at the top of the list (position 1, highest priority). Rationale: if a user reopens a task, it is likely urgent. ## Diagram ```mermaid flowchart LR EVT1["TaskAdded"]:::event EVT2["TaskReopened"]:::event AUTO["Automation:
Place task in list"]:::automation CMD["AddToTaskList"]:::command RESULT["TaskAddedToList"]:::event VIEW["ActiveTaskList"]:::view EVT1 --> AUTO EVT2 --> AUTO AUTO --> CMD CMD --> RESULT RESULT --> VIEW classDef command fill:#3b82f6,color:#fff classDef event fill:#f59e0b,color:#fff classDef view fill:#22c55e,color:#fff classDef automation fill:#8b5cf6,color:#fff ``` ## Triggering Events ### TaskAdded When a new task is created, the automation dispatches: - AddToTaskList { task_id: (from event), position: (bottom of list) } The bottom position is determined by the current number of tasks in the list plus one. ### TaskReopened When a completed task is reopened, the automation dispatches: - AddToTaskList { task_id: (from event), position: 1 } Position 1 places the task at the top (highest priority). ## Command: AddToTaskList | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | task_id | identifier | The task to place in the list | | position | number | The position to place the task at | **Rejection conditions:** - Task is already in the list (duplicate placement). ## Event: TaskAddedToList | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | task_id | identifier | The task that was placed in the list | | position | number | The position it was placed at | ## Read Model Impact **ActiveTaskList:** The task receives its authoritative position. If placed at position 1 (reopened task), existing tasks shift down. If placed at the bottom (new task), no shifts needed. ## GWT Scenarios ### Scenario: Newly created task is placed at the bottom of an empty list **Given** (prior events on the task list): - (none -- list is empty) **Trigger** (event observed by automation): - TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" } **When** (command dispatched by automation): - AddToTaskList { task_id: "task-001", position: 1 } **Then** (events produced): - TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-001", position: 1 } --- ### Scenario: Newly created task is placed at the bottom of a populated list **Given** (prior events on the task list): - TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-001", position: 1 } - TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-002", position: 2 } **Trigger** (event observed by automation): - TaskAdded { task_id: "task-003", description: "Fix the leaky faucet" } **When** (command dispatched by automation): - AddToTaskList { task_id: "task-003", position: 3 } **Then** (events produced): - TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-003", position: 3 } --- ### Scenario: Reopened task is placed at the top of the list **Given** (prior events on the task list): - TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-001", position: 1 } - TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-003", position: 2 } **Trigger** (event observed by automation): - TaskReopened { task_id: "task-002" } **When** (command dispatched by automation): - AddToTaskList { task_id: "task-002", position: 1 } **Then** (events produced): - TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-002", position: 1 } **Note:** Existing tasks shift down. After this event, task-001 is at position 2 and task-003 is at position 3. --- ### Scenario: Reject adding a task that is already in the list **Given** (prior events on the task list): - TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-001", position: 1 } - TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-002", position: 2 } **When** (command dispatched by automation): - AddToTaskList { task_id: "task-001", position: 3 } **Then** (error -- no events): - Error: "Task is already in the list"

# Slice: RemoveFromTaskList ## Pattern **Automation** ## Summary When a task is completed (TaskCompleted) or deleted (TaskDeleted), it should be removed from the ordered task list. This automation listens for those events and dispatches a RemoveFromTaskList command to the task list. - **On TaskCompleted:** The completed task is removed from the active ordering. - **On TaskDeleted:** The deleted task is removed from the active ordering (if it was active). If the task was already completed (not in the list), this is a no-op -- the rejection is handled gracefully. ## Diagram ```mermaid flowchart LR EVT1["TaskCompleted"]:::event EVT2["TaskDeleted"]:::event AUTO["Automation:
Remove task from list"]:::automation CMD["RemoveFromTaskList"]:::command RESULT["TaskRemovedFromList"]:::event VIEW["ActiveTaskList"]:::view EVT1 --> AUTO EVT2 --> AUTO AUTO --> CMD CMD --> RESULT RESULT --> VIEW classDef command fill:#3b82f6,color:#fff classDef event fill:#f59e0b,color:#fff classDef view fill:#22c55e,color:#fff classDef automation fill:#8b5cf6,color:#fff ``` ## Triggering Events ### TaskCompleted When a task is marked as done, the automation dispatches: - RemoveFromTaskList { task_id: (from event) } ### TaskDeleted When a task is permanently removed, the automation dispatches: - RemoveFromTaskList { task_id: (from event) } If the deleted task was already completed (and thus not in the task list), the command will be rejected, which the automation handles gracefully (no error surfaced to the user). ## Command: RemoveFromTaskList | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | task_id | identifier | The task to remove from the list | **Rejection conditions:** - Task is not in the list (already removed or never added). ## Event: TaskRemovedFromList | Field | Type | Description | |-------|------|-------------| | task_id | identifier | The task that was removed from the list | ## Read Model Impact **ActiveTaskList:** The task is removed from the ordered position list. Remaining tasks shift up to close the position gap, maintaining contiguous positions. ## GWT Scenarios ### Scenario: Completed task is removed from the list **Given** (prior events on the task list): - TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-001", position: 1 } - TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-002", position: 2 } - TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-003", position: 3 } **Trigger** (event observed by automation): - TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-002" } **When** (command dispatched by automation): - RemoveFromTaskList { task_id: "task-002" } **Then** (events produced): - TaskRemovedFromList { task_id: "task-002" } **Note:** After removal, task-001 remains at position 1 and task-003 shifts up to position 2. --- ### Scenario: Deleted active task is removed from the list **Given** (prior events on the task list): - TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-001", position: 1 } - TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-002", position: 2 } **Trigger** (event observed by automation): - TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-001" } **When** (command dispatched by automation): - RemoveFromTaskList { task_id: "task-001" } **Then** (events produced): - TaskRemovedFromList { task_id: "task-001" } **Note:** After removal, task-002 shifts up to position 1. --- ### Scenario: Deleted completed task is not in the list (graceful no-op) **Given** (prior events on the task list): - TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-001", position: 1 } - TaskAddedToList { task_id: "task-002", position: 2 } - TaskRemovedFromList { task_id: "task-002" } **Trigger** (event observed by automation): - TaskDeleted { task_id: "task-002" } **When** (command dispatched by automation): - RemoveFromTaskList { task_id: "task-002" } **Then** (error -- no events): - Error: "Task is not in the list" **Note:** This rejection is expected and handled gracefully by the automation. The task was already removed when it was completed. No user-visible error occurs.

input.new_string:
### Task List

The task list is a domain entity that owns the ordered sequence of all active
tasks. There is only one task list per application instance. Priority is
expressed entirely through the ordering of tasks in the list.

The task list is responsible for:

- **Position ordering:** Every active task has a unique, contiguous position in
  the list. The task list is the single source of truth for task positions.
- **Adding tasks to the list:** When a task is created or reopened, it is placed
  into the list at a specific position (bottom for new tasks, top for reopened).
- **Removing tasks from the list:** When a task is completed or deleted, it is
  removed from the list and remaining tasks shift to close the gap.
- **Reprioritizing:** When the user drags a task to a new position, the task
  list processes the move and adjusts all affected positions.

Individual tasks are created and managed independently (each task has its own
identity, description, and completion state). The task list is what gives them
their relative priority order.

1→# Slice: CompletedTaskList
     2→
     3→## Pattern
     4→
     5→**View** (State View / Read Model)
     6→
     7→## Summary
     8→
     9→A read model that projects events into a list of completed (done, non-deleted)
    10→tasks. This powers the completed tasks view where users can review finished work
    11→and reopen or delete tasks.
    12→
    13→## Diagram
    14→
    15→```mermaid
    16→flowchart LR
    17→    E1["TaskAdded"]:::event
    18→    E2["TaskCompleted"]:::event
    19→    E3["TaskReopened"]:::event
    20→    E4["TaskDescriptionChanged"]:::event
    21→    E6["TaskDeleted"]:::event
    22→    VIEW["CompletedTaskList"]:::view
    23→
    24→    E1 --> VIEW
    25→    E2 --> VIEW
    26→    E3 --> VIEW
    27→    E4 --> VIEW
    28→    E6 --> VIEW
    29→
    30→    classDef event fill:#f59e0b,color:#fff
    31→    classDef view fill:#22c55e,color:#fff
    32→```
    33→
    34→## Read Model: CompletedTaskList
    35→
    36→### Fields
    37→
    38→| Field | Type | Source Event(s) | Description |
    39→|-------|------|----------------|-------------|
    40→| tasks | list | (filtered events) | List of completed tasks |
    41→| tasks[].task_id | identifier | TaskAdded.task_id | Unique task identifier |
    42→| tasks[].description | text | TaskAdded.description, TaskDescriptionChanged.description | Current description (latest value) |
    43→
    44→### Filters
    45→
    46→- Only tasks where **completed = true** AND **deleted = false**.
    47→
    48→### Ordering
    49→
    50→- By completion time descending (most recently completed first). Completion
    51→  time is derived from the TaskCompleted event timestamp.
    52→
    53→### Event Processing Rules
    54→
    55→| Event | Effect on Read Model |
    56→|-------|---------------------|
    57→| TaskAdded | No effect (new tasks are not completed). |
    58→| TaskCompleted | Add task to completed list. |
    59→| TaskReopened | Remove task from completed list. |
    60→| TaskDescriptionChanged | Update description of matching task (if present in list). |
    61→| TaskDeleted | Remove task from completed list (if present). |
    62→
    63→### Information Completeness Check
    64→
    65→| Display Element | Source |
    66→|----------------|--------|
    67→| Task description text (with strikethrough) | TaskAdded.description / TaskDescriptionChanged.description |
    68→| Task ordering (most recent first) | TaskCompleted event timestamp |
    69→| Empty state | Derived: tasks list is empty |
    70→
    71→All fields trace to events. No missing data.
    72→
    73→## Wireframe
    74→
    75→```
    76→+--------------------------------------------------+
    77→|  [< Back to Tasks]              Completed Tasks   |
    78→+--------------------------------------------------+
    79→|                                                    |
    80→|  ~~Pick up dry cleaning~~       [Reopen]  [ x ]   |
    81→|  ~~Send the invoice~~           [Reopen]  [ x ]   |
    82→|  ~~Order new lightbulbs~~       [Reopen]  [ x ]   |
    83→|                                                    |
    84→+--------------------------------------------------+
    85→```
    86→
    87→## GWT Scenarios
    88→
    89→### Scenario: TaskAdded has no effect on completed list
    90→
    91→**Given** (current projection state):
    92→- CompletedTaskList { tasks: [] }
    93→
    94→**When** (event to process):
    95→- TaskAdded { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" }
    96→
    97→**Then** (resulting projection state):
    98→- CompletedTaskList { tasks: [] }
    99→
   100→---
   101→
   102→### Scenario: TaskCompleted adds task to the completed list
   103→
   104→**Given** (current projection state):
   105→- CompletedTaskList { tasks: [] }
   106→
   107→**When** (event to process):
   108→- TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-001" }
   109→
   110→**Then** (resulting projection state):
   111→- CompletedTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" }] }
   112→
   113→**Note:** The task's description must be resolved from prior events (TaskAdded or TaskDescriptionChanged).
   114→
   115→---
   116→
   117→### Scenario: TaskCompleted adds most recent completion to the top
   118→
   119→**Given** (current projection state):
   120→- CompletedTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" }] }
   121→
   122→**When** (event to process):
   123→- TaskCompleted { task_id: "task-002" }
   124→
   125→**Then** (resulting projection state):
   126→- CompletedTaskList { tasks: [{ task_id: "task-002", description: "Call the dentist" }, { task_id: "task-001", description: "Buy groceries" }] }
   127→
   128→**Note:** Most recently completed task appears first (descending completion time).

content.content:
1→# Domain Discovery: Todo List
     2→
     3→## Purpose
     4→
     5→The user needs a simple way to manage a prioritized list of next actions. The
     6→application lets them capture tasks, organize them by importance, and track
     7→what has been done -- all in one place with minimal friction.
     8→
     9→This is a demonstration and learning project. The engineering complexity behind
    10→it is intentional; the user-facing experience, however, should feel easy and
    11→smooth.
    12→
    13→## Actors
    14→
    15→- **User** -- A single person managing their own task list. There is no
    16→  authentication and no support for multiple users.
    17→
    18→## User Goals
    19→
    20→- See the current task list immediately upon opening the application.
    21→- Add new tasks to the list.
    22→- Edit the description of an existing task.
    23→- Mark a task as complete.
    24→- Set a completed task back to incomplete.
    25→- Delete a task that is no longer needed.
    26→- Reorder tasks by dragging and dropping them to express priority.
    27→
    28→## Business Concepts
    29→
    30→### Task
    31→
    32→A task is a single item on the user's list. It has three attributes:
    33→
    34→| Attribute        | Description                                                                 |
    35→|------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------|
    36→| Description      | A short piece of text that says what needs to be done.                      |
    37→| Completion state | Whether the task is done or not done.                                       |
    38→| Position         | Where the task sits in the priority-ordered list (higher position = higher priority). |
    39→
    40→Tasks are deliberately minimal. There are no due dates, categories, tags, or
    41→notes.
    42→
    43→### Task List
    44→
    45→The task list is a domain entity that owns the ordered sequence of all active
    46→tasks. There is only one task list per application instance. Priority is
    47→expressed entirely through the ordering of tasks in the list.
    48→
    49→The task list is responsible for:
    50→
    51→- **Position ordering:** Every active task has a unique, contiguous position in
    52→  the list. The task list is the single source of truth for task positions.
    53→- **Adding tasks to the list:** When a task is created or reopened, it is placed
    54→  into the list at a specific position (bottom for new tasks, top for reopened).
    55→- **Removing tasks from the list:** When a task is completed or deleted, it is
    56→  removed from the list and remaining tasks shift to close the gap.
    57→- **Reprioritizing:** When the user drags a task to a new position, the task
    58→  list processes the move and adjusts all affected positions.
    59→
    60→Individual tasks are created and managed independently (each task has its own
    61→identity, description, and completion state). The task list is what gives them
    62→their relative priority order.
    63→
    64→## External Integrations
    65→
    66→None. The application is entirely self-contained with no third-party services.
    67→
    68→## Workflows Identified
    69→
    70→### Task Management
    71→
    72→A single, unified workflow covering the full lifecycle of a task:
    73→
    74→- **Creation** -- The user adds a new task to the list.
    75→- **Viewing** -- The user sees all tasks and their current state.
    76→- **Editing** -- The user changes a task's description.
    77→- **Completion** -- The user marks a task as done.
    78→- **Reopening** -- The user sets a completed task back to not done.
    79→- **Reordering** -- The user drags a task to a new position to change its priority.
    80→- **Deletion** -- The user removes a task from the list entirely.
    81→
    82→## Suggested Starting Workflow
    83→
    84→**Task Management** is the only workflow, so it is the natural starting point.
    85→Within it, adding a task and viewing the task list should be modeled first,
    86→since every other activity depends on tasks existing and being visible.
    87→

QUESTION DETECTION - AskUserQuestion ENFORCEMENT

Analyze the agent's final output for patterns indicating it's blocking on user input.

## What to Look For

**BLOCKING QUESTION PATTERNS** (require AskUserQuestion tool):
- "Before I proceed, I need to know..."
- "I have a few questions:"
- Numbered/bulleted lists of questions (2+)
- "Should I... or should I..."
- "Which approach would you prefer:"
- "Would you like me to... or...?"
- Questions followed by waiting (no action taken)

**ACCEPTABLE PATTERNS** (no tool needed):
- Single clarifying question while continuing work
- Rhetorical questions in explanations ("Why does this matter?")
- Questions the agent answers itself
- Questions in code comments or documentation

## Check Tool Usage

Did the agent use the AskUserQuestion tool in this turn?

## Decision Logic

**BLOCK** if ALL of these are true:
- Output contains 2+ questions requiring user input to proceed
- Questions use blocking language ("before I", "I need to know")
- Agent has NOT taken action (appears to be waiting)
- AskUserQuestion tool was NOT used

**ALLOW** if ANY of these are true:
- AskUserQuestion was used with structured options
- Only 0-1 simple questions asked
- Questions are informational/rhetorical, not blocking
- Agent continued working and asked incidental questions

## Response Format

**To block (force tool usage):**
{"ok": false, "reason": "You asked multiple questions requiring user input but didn't use AskUserQuestion. Please reformulate using AskUserQuestion tool with structured options for: [list specific questions]"}

**To allow:**
{"ok": true}

Be specific in the reason - quote the actual questions that need reformulation.

## What to Look For

**BLOCKING QUESTION PATTERNS** (require AskUserQuestion tool):
- "Before I proceed, I need to know..."
- "I have a few questions:"
- Numbered/bulleted lists of questions (2+)
- "Should I... or should I..."
- "Which approach would you prefer:"
- "Would you like me to... or...?"
- Questions followed by waiting (no action taken)

**ACCEPTABLE PATTERNS** (no tool needed):
- Single clarifying question while continuing work
- Rhetorical questions in explanations ("Why does this matter?")
- Questions the agent answers itself
- Questions in code comments or documentation
